reason: Chen notation: weak ENTITY SET = double rectangle; double diamond is the identifying RELATIONSHIP, not the entity set.
An album is a real-world thing being modelled, i.e. an entity (type); relation/instance/attribute do not fit.
A specific record = one occurrence/instance of the entity type; table and column are schema-level, relation is the whole set.
AMAT = Hit time + Miss rate x Miss penalty (standard Hennessy & Patterson formula).
Pure ALOHA max throughput = 1/(2e) = 18.4%; nearest offered option is 18% (36.8% is slotted ALOHA). Option set is imprecise.
A collection of interrelated records/data is the textbook definition of a database.
Filters are transient; queries are persisted database objects (e.g. saved query objects in Access).
Records ordered by a key field = sequential (sorted sequential) file organization/access method.
Poorly worded stem, but the only defensible option is a database created with a DBMS package; Word/Excel files are not databases.
DML covers insert/delete/update; CREATE TABLE is DDL, so it is what DML is NOT for.
A minimal (irreducible) superkey is a candidate key; the primary key is one chosen candidate key.
A query nested inside a WHERE/HAVING clause is a subquery.
Data dictionary stores metadata (names, types, location, constraints), not physical disk device capacity; options C/D are garbled non-answers.
Data hierarchy: field < record < file < database; the field is the smallest unit processed as a single item.
A report generator produces formatted printed output; it does not update files or do data entry.
Related fields grouped together form a record.
SQL DDL removes a stored function with DROP FUNCTION.
SELECT DISTINCT eliminates duplicate rows (standard SQL); SELECT UNIQUE is only a legacy Oracle synonym, so 'All of the above' is not standard.
ALTER TRIGGER ... ENABLE/DISABLE is the command that enables or disables a trigger.
Database modification = insert, delete, update; sorting only reorders output and changes no stored data.
